label: Download Video (YT-DLP)
description: Downloads a video from a URL.

label: Download MP3 Audio (YT-DLP)
description: Extracts and downloads audio as MP3.

label: Download Metadata/Subtitles (YT-DLP)
description: Downloads metadata, subtitles, and description without the video.

label: FFmpeg Convert to MP4
description: Converts video to MP4 format.

label: FFmpeg Extract Frames
description: Extracts each frame of a video as images.

label: FFmpeg Extract Audio
description: Extracts high-quality MP3 audio from a video.

label: Record Stream (Streamlink)
description: Records livestream content to a file.

label: Download Images (Gallery-DL)
description: Downloads all images from a gallery URL.

label: Download Instagram Profile
description: Downloads posts and metadata from an Instagram profile.

label: Run Sherlock
description: Searches for username across multiple websites and outputs to CSV.

label: Run SocialScan
description: Checks availability of username or email.

label: SocialScan Output JSON
description: Exports SocialScan results to JSON.

label: Run Blackbird
description: Searches for username across social networks and exports a PDF report.

label: Run Maigret
description: Aggregates hundreds of username checks with detailed reporting.

label: Run WhatsMyName
description: Checks username presence on major platforms.

label: Run Holehe
description: Checks if an email is registered on various services.

label: GHunt Login
description: Authenticates GHunt using cookie-based login.

label: GHunt Email Search
description: Investigates a Google account using an email address.

label: GHunt Drive Search
description: Investigates a Google Drive ID for metadata.

label: H8Mail Configure API Keys
description: Generates default config file for H8Mail API keys.

label: H8Mail Run
description: Searches data breaches for email addresses.

label: Run EyeWitness (Single URL)
description: Takes screenshots of a single URL for documentation.

label: Run EyeWitness (List Mode)
description: Takes screenshots of multiple URLs.

label: Run TheHarvester
description: Collects emails, subdomains, and data from search engines.

label: Run Amass Intel
description: Performs domain reconnaissance using Amass.

label: Run Photon Crawler
description: Crawls websites to collect URLs and assets.

label: Run Sublist3r
description: Enumerates subdomains using Sublist3r.

label: Waybackpy Known URLs
description: Retrieves known archived URLs.

label: Waybackpy Oldest Snapshot
description: Retrieves the oldest available archive snapshot.

label: Run Waybackpack
description: Downloads archived snapshots of a site.

label: ExifTool Single File
description: Extracts metadata from a single file.

label: ExifTool Batch CSV
description: Extracts metadata from all files into CSV.

label: Run Metagoofil
description: Finds public documents and extracts metadata.

label: Run Xeuledoc
description: Extracts metadata from Google Docs.

label: Internet Archive Search Collection
description: Searches Internet Archive collections.

label: Internet Archive Download Items
description: Downloads multiple items from Archive.org.

label: Internet Archive Download Collection
description: Downloads all matching collection files.

label: Amass Compose Active Enumeration
description:
This runbook operationalizes Active enumeration through Amass compose workflow. using #amass, #docker. It is designed for repeatable, evidence-driven OSINT execution rather than ad-hoc one-off commands.

Execution should stay bounded to approved scope and preserve clean artifacts for downstream graphing, reporting, and profile ingestion.

  1. Prepare scope and inputs: define targets, filters, and safe execution parameters.
  2. Execute collection pipeline: run the core tooling sequence to gather high-value intelligence.
  3. Validate and persist output: normalize findings into reusable artifacts for follow-on analysis.
  • Tool Location/Source: Refer to local source docs cited in runbook-list.md; primary tooling here is #amass, #docker.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized assessment scope, operator shell access, and outbound reachability on TCP/UDP 53, TCP 80/443.
  • What attacker gain: structured intelligence outputs (assets, identities, metadata, or historical traces) that accelerate subsequent access or investigation decisions.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: enforce rate limits, monitor unusual enumeration/crawling patterns, harden exposed metadata, and alert on repeated reconnaissance behavior from single operator infrastructure.

Core Commands:

docker compose run --rm enum -active -d example.org

label: BBOT Neo4j Graph Pipeline

label: ExifTool yt-dlp Media Intelligence
command:
exiftool -a -u -g1 a.jpg
exiftool -r -w .txt -common pictures
exiftool -ee -p "$gpslatitude, $gpslongitude, $gpstimestamp" a.m2ts
yt-dlp --print filename -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" BaW_jenozKc --restrict-filenames
yt-dlp -o "%(playlist)s/%(playlist_index)s - %(title)s.%(ext)s" "https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLwiyx1dc3P2JR9N8gQaQN_BCvlSlap7re"
yt-dlp -u user -p password -P "~/MyVideos" -o "%(playlist)s/%(chapter_number)s - %(chapter)s/%(title)s.%(ext)s" "https://www.udemy.com/java-tutorial"
yt-dlp --netrc-cmd 'gpg --decrypt ~/.authinfo.gpg' 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BaW_jenozKc'
description:
This runbook operationalizes Extract image/video metadata and perform structured media acquisition with robust naming/auth workflows. using #exiftool, #yt-dlp. It is designed for repeatable, evidence-driven OSINT execution rather than ad-hoc one-off commands.

  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized assessment scope, operator shell access, and outbound reachability on TCP 80/443.
  • What attacker gain: structured intelligence outputs (assets, identities, metadata, or historical traces) that accelerate subsequent access or investigation decisions.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: enforce rate limits, monitor unusual enumeration/crawling patterns, harden exposed metadata, and alert on repeated reconnaissance behavior from single operator infrastructure.

Core Commands:

exiftool -a -u -g1 a.jpg
exiftool -r -w .txt -common pictures
exiftool -ee -p "$gpslatitude, $gpslongitude, $gpstimestamp" a.m2ts
yt-dlp --print filename -o "%(title)s.%(ext)s" BaW_jenozKc --restrict-filenames
yt-dlp -o "%(playlist)s/%(playlist_index)s - %(title)s.%(ext)s" "https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLwiyx1dc3P2JR9N8gQaQN_BCvlSlap7re"
yt-dlp -u user -p password -P "~/MyVideos" -o "%(playlist)s/%(chapter_number)s - %(chapter)s/%(title)s.%(ext)s" "https://www.udemy.com/java-tutorial"
yt-dlp --netrc-cmd 'gpg --decrypt ~/.authinfo.gpg' 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BaW_jenozKc'

  1. Prepare scope and inputs: define targets, filters, and safe execution parameters.
  2. Execute collection pipeline: run the core tooling sequence to gather high-value intelligence.
  3. Validate and persist output: normalize findings into reusable artifacts for follow-on analysis.
  • Tool Location/Source: Refer to local source docs cited in runbook-list.md; primary tooling here is #katana.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized assessment scope, operator shell access, and outbound reachability on TCP 80/443.
  • What attacker gain: structured intelligence outputs (assets, identities, metadata, or historical traces) that accelerate subsequent access or investigation decisions.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: enforce rate limits, monitor unusual enumeration/crawling patterns, harden exposed metadata, and alert on repeated reconnaissance behavior from single operator infrastructure.

Core Commands:

katana -u https://www.hackerone.com -mdc 'status_code == 200'
katana -u https://example.com -jsonl -eof raw,body
katana -u https://example.com -no-scope -store-response
